steps.title: Convert the square root to a fractional exponent
steps.content: A square root is the same as raising to the power $\frac{1}{2}$: $$\sqrt{x^{153}} = \left(x^{153}\right)^{1/2} = x^{153 \cdot \frac{1}{2}} = x^{153/2}.$$

steps.title: Eliminate incorrect choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: $x^{\frac{2}{153}}$ — this would be the $153$rd root squared, not the square root of $x^{153}$ — eliminate.
  • Choice B: $x^{\frac{153}{2}}$ — matches the derivation above — keep.
  • Choice C: $x^{151}$ — the exponent would need to be $153/2 = 76.5$, not $151$ — eliminate.
  • Choice D: $x^{306}$ — squaring $x^{153}$ gives $x^{306}$, but we are taking the square root, not squaring — eliminate.
That matches Choice B — $x^{\frac{153}{2}}$.
